sampleTestCases.explanation:
Your task is to answer QQQ queries. For the ithi^{th}ith query:
You are given an integer SiS_iSi‚Äã and you need to find a mountain with sum SiS_iSi‚Äã.
Input Format

The first line contains three space-separated integers NNN, MMM, and QQQ, the dimensions of the matrix and the number of queries.
The second line contains QQQ space-separated integers S1,S2,‚Ä¶,SQS_1,S_2,\ldots,S_QS1‚Äã,S2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,SQ‚Äã, denoting each query.
Output Format

For each query, if no possible mountain with given sum exists, output ‚àí1-1‚àí1.
Otherwise, output two lines:

The first line contains two space-separated integers PPP,KKK (1‚â§P‚â§N,P+K‚àí1‚â§N)(1 \leq P \leq N,P+K-1 \leq N)(1‚â§P‚â§N,P+K‚àí1‚â§N);
The second line contains KKK space-separated integers L1,L2,‚Ä¶,LK(1‚â§Li‚â§M)L_1,L_2,\ldots,L_K(1 \leq L_i \leq M)L1‚Äã,L2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,LK‚Äã(1‚â§Li‚Äã‚â§M).



If multiple mountains satisfy the condition, you may print any.
